message:
🌱 Add localai: Allow sideloading backends on filesystem mission (#1177)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "localai-5917-allow-sideloading-backends-on-filesystem",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "localai: Allow sideloading backends on filesystem",
+    "description": "Allow sideloading backends on filesystem. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current localai deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your localai version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n localai -l app.kubernetes.io/name=localai\nhelm list -n localai 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working localai installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review localai configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant localai configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n localai -l app.kubernetes.io/name=localai\nkubectl get configmap -n localai -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=localai\n```\nI am trying to package LocalAI to archlinux, so LocalAI can be installed and run directly on archlinux with its files managed by pacman. But I find it is hard to package those backends like llama-cpp, piper, bark, etc."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Allow sideloading backends on filesystem",
+        "description": "This PR is related to https://github.com/mudler/LocalAI/issues/5917\n\nThis allows to call `local-ai backends install` on local paths. For instance:\n\n```\nlocal-ai backends install path/to/backend\n```\n\nNote, to specify a backends path, set `--backends-path` accordingly, for example:\n\n```\nlocal-ai backends install --backends-path /var/lib/local-ai/backends path/to/backend/just/built\n```\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n localai -l app.kubernetes.io/name=localai\nkubectl get events -n localai --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Allow sideloading backends on filesystem\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "This PR is related to https://github.com/mudler/LocalAI/issues/5917\n\nThis allows to call `local-ai backends install` on local paths. For instance:\n\n```\nlocal-ai backends install path/to/backend\n```\n\nNote, to specify a backends path, set `--backends-path` accordingly, for example:\n\n```\nlocal-ai backends install --backends-path /var/lib/local-ai/backends path/to/backend/just/built\n```",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "localai",
+      "community",
+      "llm-serving",
+      "feature"
+    ],

+    ],

+      "feature"
+    ],

+      "repo": "https://github.com/mudler/LocalAI",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/mudler/LocalAI/pull/5962"
+    },

+    "comments": 13,

+  },

+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
